ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "time_est" TEXT /* Time of data collection in Eastern Standard Time (EST) */,
  "temperature_f" REAL /* Temperature in Fahrenheit */,
  "dew_point_f" REAL /* Dew Point in Fahrenheit */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Relative humidity as a percentage */,
  "sea_level_pressure_in" REAL /* Sea Level Pressure in inches of mercury */,
  "visibility_mph" REAL /* Visibility in miles per hour */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Wind direction (e.g., Calm, North, Variable, NE) */,
  "wind_speed_mph" TEXT /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "gust_speed_mph" TEXT /* Gust speed in miles per hour */,
  "precipitation_in" REAL /* Precipitation in inches */,
  "events" TEXT /* Weather events (e.g., Fog, Rain) */,
  "conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions (e.g., Overcast, Light Drizzle) */,
  "wind_dir_degrees" INTEGER /* Wind direction in degrees */,
  "date_utc" TEXT /* Date and time of data collection in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the weather station */,
  "elevation_m" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station in meters */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "data_version" TEXT /* Version of the weather data */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the weather data (e.g., sensor, satellite) */,
  "quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for the data (e.g., good, suspect, bad) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the data entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the data entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "elevation_m" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station in meters */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the station (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_events" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of weather event (e.g., fog, rain, drizzle) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the weather event */,
  "start_time_utc" TEXT /* Start time of the weather event in UTC */,
  "end_time_utc" TEXT /* End time of the weather event in UTC */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the weather event (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, data analyst, administrator) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object containing the permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the `users` table */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the `roles` table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "weather_data_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the `weather_data` table */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the data */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the version was created */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
